channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: NHL fines Golden Knights and Coach Tortorella for media violations after Game 6
item.description: The Vegas Golden Knights were penalized by the NHL following their series-clinching 5-1 win over the Anaheim Ducks. The team was docked a second-round draft pick and coach John Tortorella was fined $1...

item.title: Tanzania urges banks to expand access as financial sector grows steadily
item.description: The Tanzanian government has expressed satisfaction with the steady growth and stability of the country’s financial sector, urging banks and financial institutions to increase access for Tanzanians wh...

item.title: Senators report pressure and threats amid talks of changing Senate leadership
item.description: Just days after Alan Peter Cayetano assumed the position of Senate President, concerns are emerging over potential attempts to replace him again. According to a report by Ian Cruz on '24 Oras,' Cayeta...

item.title: US and Nigerian Forces Conduct Operation to Kill ISIS Deputy Abu Bilal al-Minuki
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced that Abu Bilal al-Minuki, considered the second highest leader in ISIS worldwide, was killed during a joint operation by American and Nigerian forces. According to ...

item.title: Philippines Facilitates Quiet Diplomatic Progress Between Cambodia and Thailand at ASEAN Summit
item.description: During the 48th ASEAN Summit held in Cebu, the Philippines played a key role as a diplomatic convenor in fostering dialogue between Cambodia and Thailand. Unlike previous externally pressured agreemen...

item.title: Alberta and Ottawa reach energy accord amid pipeline and independence debates
item.description: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith and Prime Minister Mark Carney announced a new energy accord aimed at facilitating the construction of a bitumen pipeline to the West Coast. Under the agreement, Alberta...

item.title: Smoggie Queens: A Quirky, Heartfelt Sitcom Celebrating Queer Life in Middlesbrough
item.description: Smoggie Queens is a uniquely eccentric sitcom set in Middlesbrough, inspired by the life of creator and star Phil Dunning. The show blends local Teesside humour with queer culture references, producin...

item.title: Hate Speech in India: Legal Measures Alone Cannot Solve the Problem
item.description: Since 2014, hateful speech targeting Muslim and Christian minorities in India has become increasingly common and normalized in public life. Such speech appears on political platforms, religious discou...

item.title: Philippines lifts ban on Spanish pork imports after ASF safety measures recognized
item.description: MANILA — The Department of Agriculture (DA) has officially lifted its ban on pork and other swine products from Spain after recognizing the country's regionalization measures for African swine fever (...

item.title: Hearts and Celtic face winner-takes-all clash in Scottish Premiership finale
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has reached a dramatic conclusion, with Hearts and Celtic set for a decisive final match. Hearts, under Derek McInnes, have led the league for most of the season an...

item.title: Stephen Colbert Announces Star-Studded Lineup for Final Week of ‘Late Show’
item.description: Stephen Colbert is preparing for his final week on air at “The Late Show” with a series of special episodes featuring prominent guests. The CBS late-night program’s schedule for May 18 includes a Mond...

item.title: Legal Experts Question Trump's $10 Billion IRS Lawsuit and Proposed Settlement Fund
item.description: A recent segment on MS NOW's 'All In' highlighted concerns over former President Donald Trump's $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS. According to host Chris Hayes, the lawsuit could serve as a vehicle...

item.title: Power grids in Luzon and Visayas under red alert, rotational outages expected
item.description: For the third day in a row, power grids in Luzon and the Visayas were placed under red alert, causing rotational blackouts lasting two to three hours. In Luzon, the National Grid Corp. of the Philippi...

item.title: Experts Warn Gas Tax Holiday Would Provide Minimal Savings and Harm Infrastructure Funding
item.description: As gas prices remain high across the United States, President Donald Trump has proposed a temporary suspension of the federal gas tax, an idea also being considered in Congress. However, experts argue...

item.title: INEC Receives and Begins Verification of Party Membership Registers Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: INEC don confirm say all the 22 registered political parties for Nigeria don submit their membership registers as required by the Electoral Act 2026. According to statement wey Mohammed Haruna, the ch...

item.title: CPA Roble Said Nuno Named CEO and Commission Secretary at CRA
item.description: The Commission on Revenue Allocation (CRA) has officially appointed CPA Roble Said Nuno as its new Chief Executive Officer and Commission Secretary, following a competitive recruitment process that at...

item.title: Man Encounters King Cobra Inside Hotel Toilet, Safely Removed by Staff
item.description: A man visiting a resort in Don Salvador Benedicto, Philippines, had a shock of his life when he discovered a king cobra resting inside a traditional toilet. The highly venomous snake had apparently en...

item.title: Nottingham couple receive suspended sentences for arranging marriages of their underage sons in Pakistan
item.description: A couple from Nottingham have received suspended prison sentences after taking their underage sons to Pakistan to enter into marriage. The parents, in their 50s and 40s, travelled with their teenage s...

item.title: Gwadar University VC and three others missing after suspected abduction near Mastung
item.description: In a worrying development from Balochistan, the vice chancellor of Gwadar University, Prof Dr Abdul Razzaq Sabir, along with Pro-Vice Chancellor Prof Dr Syed Manzoor Ahmed, staff officer Irshad Ahmed,...

item.title: Lahore High Court Rules No Super Tax on Capital Gains from Inherited Property
item.description: The Lahore High Court (LHC) has recently delivered a significant judgement regarding taxation of inherited property. A two-judge bench, led by Justice Jawad Hassan and Justice Sardar Akbar Ali, declar...
